# src

Rust source for `gg-walker-wasm`, the Tier-C "interpreter-embed" walker in the
Betti-to-WASM pipeline. It executes the property-carried subset of GG
orchestration topologies (the IR that `gnosis_polyglot` extracts on the host at
build time) and traps fail-closed on anything outside that certified subset, so
the wasm lane never returns an answer that could diverge from the reference
gnexec walker. The same crate builds as a program-independent wasm32 cdylib and
as an rlib consumed natively by `gnosis-polyglot` for package-time admission
sweeps.

## Key Files

| File | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `lib.rs` | The walker itself (~1,900 lines). Defines the `Topology`/`Node`/`Edge` IR types, envelope parsing (`run_envelope_bytes` for v1 `{topology, input}`, v2 `+ helpers`, v3 `+ budget/resume` cursors), validation entry points (`validate_topology`, `validate_program`, JSON variants), and the walk executors (`run_topology`, `run_program`, `run_program_bounded`). The module doc comment is the normative contract for envelope shapes, certified node labels, expression forms, and decline rules. |
| `wasm.rs` | The wasm32 export surface: the gnosis json-v1 ABI (`memory`, `gnosis_alloc`, `main(ptr, len)`, `gnosis_result_len`, `gnosis_free` as a deliberate no-op). Zero imports; a decline becomes a real wasm trap via `panic = "abort"`. Mirrors `open-source/swerve/src/wasm.rs`. |

## Usage

Built as a standalone wasm32 module from the crate root:

```sh
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release
```

Hosts (e.g. `gnosis/src/wasm-execution-bridge.ts`, swerve) instantiate the
module and call `main` with a JSON envelope; `gnosis_polyglot::aot_gg_embed`
packages the prebuilt cdylib with a `<artifactId>.topology.json` sidecar. See
the parent `../README.md` for build, coverage, and test details.
